methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vuecason is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Dival Banerjee that has raised $3M+. The comparable universe includes 9 private robotics and manufacturing peers (1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and others) and 1 public company (277810.KQ), though formal peer median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow robotics subsector.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach, applying a 2.5x multiplier to the $3M capital raised proxy. This step-up reflects typical progression economics from seed to next stage and yields an implied valuation of $8M. A conservative band around this central estimate, accounting for execution risk and limited public market anchors, ranges from $6M (15% haircut) to $9M (12.5% premium).

The $8M median valuation reflects reasonable downside and upside parameters for a seed-stage robotics company with demonstrated early traction. Key uncertainties include the absence of tighter peer metrics at identical stage and valuation tier, reliance on a single triangulation method, and the nascent nature of the sector which limits long-tail public market precedent. Further validation would require additional seed-stage robotics comparables or revenue metrics if available in future rounds.
